original_image_tags: A line graph titled "Titan IV Random-Attitude Failures through 300 sec". The y-axis is labeled "Percent in 5-deg sector (%)" on a logarithmic scale from 0.1 to 100. The x-axis is labeled "Angle From Flight Path (deg)" from 0 to 180. The graph plots several data series and theoretical curves. A legend indicates symbols for "Breakup q-alpha in deg-lb/ft²": inverted triangle for "no breakup", solid circle for "20,000", open circle for "10,000", and open square for "5,000". A second legend indicates theoretical curves for "B = 1,000" with solid lines for "A = 2.00", "A = 2.95", "A = 3.25", and "A = 3.50". All plotted lines show a decreasing trend as the angle increases.

elements.description: A line graph with markers titled 'Titan IV Random-Attitude Failures through 300 sec'. The Y-axis is labeled 'Percent in 5-deg sector (%)' and ranges from 0.1 to 100. The X-axis is labeled 'Angle From Flight Path (deg)' and ranges from 0 to 180. The legend includes a section titled 'Breakup q-alpha in deg-lb/ft²' with markers for 'no breakup', '20,000', '10,000', and '5,000'. Another section is titled 'B = 1,000' and shows four line styles for 'A = 2.00', 'A = 2.95', 'A = 3.25', and 'A = 3.50'. The graph contains several plotted lines and marker series. The caption below the graph reads 'Figure 27. Titan Simulation Results with B = 1,000'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the title, axis labels and ranges, legend categories and specific values, and overall visual content, with no contradictions on factual visual elements.
